content.content:
content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: Request a credit from the Xfinity Assistant

  - url: https://www.xfinity.com/support/articles/credit-xfinity-assistant

  - source: Xfinity

  - domain: xfinity.com


  ## Content


  # Request a credit from the Xfinity Assistant


  Xfinity provides an account-based credit request process through Xfinity Assistant.
  The process is available to a primary account holder or account manager who signs
  in through the Xfinity app or xfinity.com and opens Xfinity Assistant.


  A customer may be able to request a credit for several different types of charges
  or service issues. The listed reasons include:


  - Early termination fee

  - Installation fee

  - In-home service visit fee

  - Service outage

  - Video or On Demand purchase or rental issue


  These are separate reasons for a credit request. Selecting a reason starts a review
  for that category; the existence of one kind of service or billing problem does
  not by itself establish that a different credit reason applies.


  To start:


  1. Sign in as the primary account holder or an account manager.

  2. Open Xfinity Assistant.

  3. Ask to request a credit.

  4. Select the option that matches the charge or service issue.

  5. Answer the follow-up questions used to determine whether the request meets the
  requirements for that credit reason.


  Xfinity Assistant checks the request against the selected issue and the account
  information. If the request qualifies, the Assistant confirms that a credit will
  be issued. A new bill-credit request may take up to 10 minutes to appear on the
  account.


  A customer should use the reason that corresponds to the issue being reviewed. For
  example, a service outage and an in-home service visit fee are both listed credit
  reasons, but they are not the same request category.

  '
metadata: {}
title: Request a credit from the Xfinity Assistant
url: https://www.xfinity.com/support/articles/credit-xfinity-assistant

content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: Dispute a charge on your Xfinity bill

  - url: https://www.xfinity.com/support/articles/dispute-bill

  - source: Xfinity

  - domain: xfinity.com


  ## Content


  # Dispute a charge on your Xfinity bill


  Xfinity has a separate process for disputing certain charges that appear on a bill.
  A billing dispute is subject to its own timing and charge-category rules.


  A charge must be disputed within 120 days of when it appears on the bill.


  For Xfinity Internet, TV, Voice, or Home, the dispute process covers certain billing
  problems. One example is a service that the customer did not order, such as an added
  package or other service that was not requested.


  Not every billing concern is handled through the general dispute process. Xfinity
  also lists categories that cannot be disputed through this route. One example is
  a promotion that ended on schedule.


  Before using the dispute process, the customer should identify the charge that appears
  on the bill and determine whether that charge belongs to a disputable category.
  A nearby credit or adjustment process may exist for another type of issue.


  The dispute route therefore should not be treated as a universal replacement for
  every Xfinity credit process. A service-outage credit, a credit request for an in-home
  service visit fee, and a general bill-charge dispute are distinct types of account
  issues and may have different requirements.

  '
metadata: {}
title: Dispute a charge on your Xfinity bill
url: https://www.xfinity.com/support/articles/dispute-bill
